message:
fix(security): add domain separators to SHA-256 hashes in incident_lab and time_travel

Concatenating raw byte fields without separators in SHA-256 digests
allows second-preimage collisions where different (seq, payload) pairs
produce the same digest if their byte concatenation is identical (e.g.
seq=0x0102 + payload "AB" vs seq=0x01 + payload 0x02"AB"). This is the
same class of vulnerability fixed in prior commits for other modules.

Changes:
- incident_lab: insert "|" separator between seq bytes and payload_hex
  bytes in replay trace digest computation, and after each event
- time_travel: insert "|" separators between all fields in
  WorkflowSnapshot digest (frame_index, clock_tick, input_hash, decision
  digest) and in deterministic_decision (seed, tick, input)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
